subject: Geography
title: The Projection Problem
description: A 60-second lesson on The Projection Problem.

hook.text: You cannot flatten a sphere without distorting something. Every flat map lies.

buildup.text: Try peeling an orange and pressing the skin flat. It tears, stretches, and warps.

discovery.text: Map projections choose what to distort: area, shape, distance, or direction.

twist.text: No projection can preserve all four. Every map sacrifices truth somewhere.

climax.text: The projection you choose reveals what you value—navigation, fairness, or aesthetics.

punchline.text: Every flat map is a compromise. The question is which lie you prefer.

quiz.question: Why can't any flat map be perfectly accurate?
options: You can't flatten a sphere without distortion
